page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs, operating with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% in stress-ng multi-core tests. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25%. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is strong, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations, all scoring in the top 25%. However, text generation using large 70B LLM models is a notable bottleneck, registering in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2d-standard-48 offers a high density of dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, which eliminates resource contention at the core level. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a cost-effective profile for workloads that require substantial compute capacity without the premium pricing of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances.

This server is designed for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference. It is also highly capable for image processing, ray tracing, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from dedicated physical cores.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7B13 x86_64 processor. Equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, it provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU capabilities, ranking in the top 10% in stress-ng tests, alongside strong software compilation, database operations, and image processing performance. However, large-block memory bandwidth remains average, and text generation for large 70B language models is poor. This instance offers high resource density and cost efficiency by providing dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database workloads, and static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose instance with 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance, ideal for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 features 48 AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for databases and compilation.
tagline: A dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC server with balanced memory, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for compute-heavy and database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores with 1.0 thread per core
192 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance ranking in the top 10%
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Strong performance in software compilation and database operations
Suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and databases
